---
title: "Course Syllabus"
format:
  pdf:
    documentclass: article
    papersize: a4
    geometry:
      - margin=2.5cm
    number-sections: false
    colorlinks: true
    linkcolor: "ju-purple"
    urlcolor: "ju-purple"
    include-in-header:
      text: |
        \usepackage{xcolor}
        \usepackage{colortbl}
        \usepackage{longtable}
        \usepackage{array}
        \definecolor{ju-purple}{RGB}{150,27,129}
        \definecolor{ju-darkblue}{RGB}{0,56,101}
        \definecolor{lightgray}{gray}{0.9}
        \renewcommand{\familydefault}{\sfdefault}
        \renewcommand{\maketitle}{}
        \usepackage{titlesec}
        \titleformat{\section}{\Large\bfseries\color{ju-darkblue}}{}{0em}{}
        \titleformat{\subsection}{\large\bfseries\color{ju-darkblue}}{}{0em}{}
        \titleformat{\subsubsection}{\normalsize\bfseries\color{ju-purple}}{}{0em}{}
lang: en
---

\thispagestyle{empty} 

```{=latex}
{\Large\bfseries\color{ju-purple} COURSE SYLLABUS}

\vspace{0.3cm}

{\LARGE\bfseries Mechatronics, 7.5 credits}

\vspace{0.1cm}

{\large\itshape Mekatronik, 7{,}5 högskolepoäng}

\vspace{0.8cm}

\begin{tabular}{@{}p{3cm}p{3.5cm}p{3.5cm}p{4cm}@{}}
Course code: & TMRK16 & Level: & First cycle \\
Established: & 2025-02-01 & Field of education: & Technology \\
Revised: & 2025-10-29 & Subject group: & Mechanical Engineering \\
Valid from: & 2026-01-19 & Specialisation: & G1F \\
 & & Main field: & Mechanical Engineering \\
\end{tabular}

\vspace{0.5cm}
\noindent\rule{\textwidth}{0.4pt}
```

## Learning Outcomes

After completing the course, the student shall:

### Knowledge and understanding

- demonstrate knowledge of linear voltage regulation, batteries, battery charging, and its environmental impact, power dimensioning, and energy consumption
- demonstrate knowledge of commonly used sensors and actuators in electromechanical systems
- demonstrate knowledge of different types of communication between electromechanical systems
- demonstrate in-depth knowledge of fundamental concepts in electronics such as: resistance, voltage, current, capacitance, power, Ohm's law, Kirchhoff's laws, and more

### Skills and abilities

- demonstrate the ability to use and develop code for microcontrollers to implement electromechanical systems
- demonstrate the ability to use multimeters and oscilloscopes to measure and troubleshoot electromechanical systems
- demonstrate the ability to realise simple circuit boards through PCB circuit construction and soldering of components
- demonstrate the ability to select appropriate sensors and actuators to implement electromechanical systems
- demonstrate the ability to implement fundamental control and regulation in electromechanical systems

## Content

The course covers fundamental concepts in electronics and the use of electromechanical components to create prototypes of electromechanical systems.

The course includes the following elements:

- Electrical concepts and DC circuit calculations
- Resistors, capacitors, diodes, semiconductor technology, and transistors as switches
- Instrument knowledge
- Microcontroller programming
- Sensors/transducers for measuring position, motion, force, temperature
- Control of various motors/actuators, e.g., DC, servo, stepper, and brushless motors
- Various communication protocols for hardware-level communication, component interconnection
- Wireless technology for communication and control
- Implementation of control and regulation
- Battery technology
- Control systems for production
- Control systems for embedded products

## Teaching Methods

Teaching is conducted through lectures, exercises or demonstrations, and laboratory sessions.

Teaching is conducted in Swedish but English may occur.

## Prerequisites

General eligibility and completion of courses in Product Development and CAD, 7.5 credits, Scientific Programming, 7.5 credits, and Analysis and Linear Algebra, 15 credits (or equivalent knowledge).

## Examination and Grading

The course is graded 5, 4, 3, or U (Fail).

| Examination component | Credits | Grade |
|:--|:--|:--|
| Project^1^ | 4.5 credits | 5/4/3/U |
| Laboratory work^2^ | 3 credits | P/U |

^1^ Determines the final course grade, which is issued only when all components are approved.\
^2^ Project and laboratory work are carried out in groups.

## Course Literature

Note that the course literature may change up to eight weeks before the course starts.

### Reference (not required to purchase)

Title: Mechatronics\
Author: William Bolton\
ISBN: 9789353065881

Title: Practical Electronics for Inventors\
Author: Paul Scherz, Simon Monk\
ISBN: 9781259587542
